task_completion.description: A pipeline or channel named 'PHI_Deidentification_Pipeline' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A channel named 'PHI_Deidentification_Pipeline' is visible in an integration engine (like Mirth Connect) or a script with this exact name is created.

task_completion.description: The pipeline is configured to read HL7 files from the input directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Source/Reader settings in the GUI show '/home/ga/hl7_input/' as the directory, or script code explicitly defines this path as the input source.

task_completion.description: The pipeline is configured to write cleaned messages to the output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Destination/Writer settings in the GUI show '/home/ga/hl7_output/' as the directory, or script code explicitly defines this path as the output destination.

task_completion.description: The pipeline contains logic to replace PID-5 and PID-11, and remove PID-13 and PID-19.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Transformation steps, mappings, or code explicitly assigning 'ANONYMOUS^PATIENT' to PID-5, 'REMOVED^^REMOVED^XX^00000' to PID-11, and assigning empty values (or removing) PID-13 and PID-19.

task_completion.description: The channel is deployed and processes the sample files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The integration engine dashboard shows the channel as 'Deployed' with at least 3 messages received/sent, or the terminal shows the pipeline script executing successfully.

task_completion.description: The output HL7 files contain the de-identified data and no original PHI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal commands (e.g., cat, grep) or a text editor showing the contents of files in '/home/ga/hl7_output/'. The PID segment must show 'ANONYMOUS^PATIENT' and 'REMOVED^^REMOVED^XX^00000', and must NOT contain original names (JOHNSON, MARTINEZ, THOMPSON), SSNs, or original addresses.

integrity.description: The agent created a reusable pipeline/channel rather than manually editing the 3 specific files.
integrity.visual_evidence: A script or Mirth Connect channel is configured with general rules for PID-5, PID-11, PID-13, PID-19, rather than hardcoding the replacement of 'JOHNSON' or other specific patient data.

integrity.description: The output files were generated by the pipeline, not manually created.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent executes the pipeline or deploys the channel, and the files appear in the output directory as a result, rather than being created with `echo` or a text editor.

integrity.description: The rest of the HL7 message remains intact.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output files still contain valid HL7 segments (MSH, EVN, PV1) and fields separated by '|', not just the modified PID segment.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input directory: /home/ga/hl7_input. Output directory: /home/ga/hl7_output. Channel name: PHI_Deidentification_Pipeline. Required replacements: PID-5 must be 'ANONYMOUS^PATIENT'. PID-11 must be 'REMOVED^^REMOVED^XX^00000'. PID-13 and PID-19 must be empty/removed. Forbidden strings (original PHI that must not appear in output): JOHNSON, MARTINEZ, THOMPSON, 987654321, 123456789, 456789012, 456 OAK AVENUE, 789 PINE STREET, 321 MAPLE DRIVE. There are exactly 3 input files.
